Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 22 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: ここが、事件のあった駐車場か。
English: So this is the lot where it all happened?

Japanese: まだ、捜査中みたいですね‥‥
English: Looks like they're still investigating...

Japanese: （そういえば、検事局に 来るのって、初めてだな‥‥）
English: (Funny that my first visit to the Prosecutor's Office should be like this...)

Japanese: みなさーん、ゴクローさまですッ！
English: Hey everyone! Keep up the good work!

Japanese: こ‥‥こらこら！ ナニしてるんだよ！
English: H-hey! What are you thinking?

Japanese: あ、ホラ。あたしも３年後には シゴト仲間になるわけですから。
English: Well, they are going to be my coworkers three years from now after all.

Japanese: ここはひとつ、ごアイサツを‥‥
English: No harm in saying hello...

Japanese: あのね。弁護士には、現場を 捜査するケンリはないんだよ。
English: Actually, there is. You know attorneys aren't supposed to examine crime scenes?

Japanese: あんまりハデに目立つと‥‥
English: I'm trying to not stand out too much, here, see?

Japanese: ヘイ！
English: Hey there!

Japanese: それでジミを気取ってるつもりかい ‥‥カウボーイ！
English: You 'specting to go unnoticed here, pardner?

Japanese: か、かうぼーい‥‥？
English: P-Pardner?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: What do we have here?

Japanese: 牧場をはぐれたバンビーナが、 おイタをしてるようだよ。
English: Looks like a bambina got loose from the ranch and is up to no good!

Japanese: しっかり、ナワでつないで もらいたいものだね。
English: Folks gotta learn to keep them dogies tied down, pardner.

Japanese: ざ‥‥ザイモンさん！
English: M-Mr. Marshall!

Japanese: （ざいもん‥‥ このアメリカ人の名前か？）
English: (Marshall? Looks more like a sheriff to me...)

Japanese: いいかい、バンビーナ。 キミのキモチはわかるさ。
English: Lookie here, bambina. I know how you feel.

Japanese: だがしかし。ここはオレたちの ゴールドラッシュ‥‥わかるだろ？
English: But this is my gang's gold strike, see?

Japanese: ごーるどらっしゅ‥‥？
English: Strike...?

Japanese: “証拠”というおタカラが眠る、 ユメの金鉱‥‥ナワバリ、なのさ。
English: This is our claim, our territory. And the goldmine is... evidence.

Japanese: 荒らそうってハラなら‥‥ タダじゃすまないね、カウボーイ！
English: If you're fixin' to mess with what's ours... You'll regret it, pardner!

Japanese: ‥‥荒野のサボテンが見るユメ。 アンタも見てみたいかい‥‥？
English: You know what dreams the cacti out in the desert dream? You want to?

Japanese: （イミがわかんないよ！）
English: (What's this guy talking about!?)

Japanese: とにかく、おウチへ帰るコトさ。 ‥‥あばよ、バンビーナ！
English: You head along home now. Happy trails, bambina!

Japanese: あの。今の宇宙じ‥‥外国人、 きみの知り合いなの？
English: Was that uh, hombre, a friend of yours?

Japanese: え。‥‥あ、はい。まあ‥‥
English: Uh... kind of... sort of... Yeah. He's a detective.

Japanese: （捜査官‥‥というより、 保安官だよな、アレ‥‥）
English: (Who thinks he's a sheriff from the Wild West it seems.)

Japanese: さあ！　こうなったら、 調べまくっちゃいましょうよ！
English: Well, no time to waste! Let's get hunting for clues!

Japanese: ええと、これはナニかな‥‥
English: Hmm... I wonder what this is?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥カウボーイ。 おとなしく帰る気はないようだ。
English: Well, pardner. Looks like you got no intention of going home quietly.

Japanese: （さっきの保安官‥‥！）
English: (The sheriff!)

Japanese: 言ったハズだよ。 ここは、オレたちのナワバリさ。
English: Like I said before, this here's our claim.

Japanese: おとなしく帰らないと‥‥ 弾丸を噛むコトになるさ。
English: You'd best be moseying along. Unless you're fixing to bite the bullet.
(Gah! Scary!)

Japanese: あ、あの！　せめて‥‥ あの赤いクルマの持ち主だけでも！
English: C-could you just tell us one thing...? Who owns that car?

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥ ハナのきく子馬ちゃんだね。
English: Well, well. The little filly's got a good nose on her!

Japanese: 死体をくわえた、赤いジャジャ馬の ご主人を知りたい、ってワケだ。
English: You want to know who rides that red mustang with the body in her saddle, eh?

Japanese: お‥‥おねがいしますっ！
English: Please!

Japanese: ‥‥オーライ。いいだろうさ。 マチネーにはちょうどいい時間だ。
English: No problem, pardner. 'Bout time for vittles anyway.

Japanese: １２階の、このサルーンに 行ってみるといい。
English: Get yourself to the saloon up on the 12th floor of the Prospector's Office.

Japanese: 今なら、ウマいエールに ありつけるかも、だぜ。
English: Might just find you a cervesa you like.

Japanese: （どうもこのヒト、国境と時代を 飛び越えてるんだよな‥‥）
English: (Prospector's Office? Where does this guy think he is? And when, for that matter!?)

Japanese: マチネー、サルーン、エール‥‥ あとで調べておくこと、と。
English: Note to self: look up vittles, saloon, cervesa.

Japanese: （上級検事執務室、１２０２号。 行ってみるか！）
English: (Maybe we should check out room 1202... the High Prosecutor's Office!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥クルマのまわりは 立入禁止にさせてもらうよ。
English: In any case, stay away from the car.

Japanese: 遊びたけりゃ、このへんに しておくことだね。
English: You can look around here all you like, just keep your paws off our claim.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥しかたないな）
English: (Right... great.)

Japanese: よおし！　このへんだけでも、 シッカリ調べましょう！
English: Great! Maybe there are some clues around here, Mr. Wright! Let's check it out!

Japanese: これは‥‥サイフ、かな。
English: What's this? A wallet?

Japanese: あのー、すみませーん！ おまわりさーん！
English: Um, excuse me! Officer!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょっとちょっと！ ナニしてるんですか、成歩堂さん！
English: W-w-waaaait! What are you doing, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: な、ナニって、サイフを拾ったら、 ちゃんとケーサツに‥‥
English: What am I doing? I just found this wallet, so I'm handing it over to the police...

Japanese: ‥‥はああああ。
English: I don't believe it!

Japanese: いいですか。現場で拾ったものは 《証拠物件》なんです。
English: This is real basic: anything at a crime scene is evidence!

Japanese: もっと、カガク的に行きましょう！ ホラ。ポケットにしまうしまう。
English: Let's be scientific about this, please! Just put it in your pocket.

Japanese: か、かがくてき、って‥‥ （ドロボーにされちまったぞ‥‥）
English: H-how is that scientific? (Sounds like theft to me!)

Japanese: 《サイフ》をポケットに ぎゅっとネジこまれた。
English: Wallet hastily stuffed into pocket.

Japanese: さあ、あたしの出番ですね！ すでにこのトシにして、もう！
English: I'm called to duty already, and at my tender age!

Japanese: トクベツに、《証拠品》を詳しく 調べる方法を教えちゃいますね！
English: Here, I'll teach you the trick to examining evidence in detail, okay?

Japanese: （異様にうれしそうだな‥‥ 目がギラギラかがやいているぞ）
English: (By the way her eyes are sparkling I can tell she's been waiting for this...)

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、サッソク。 《法廷記録》を見てください！
English: Okay, okay, now. Look at the Court Record!

Japanese: 《証拠品》は、タテからヨコから ジックリ見るのがダイジなんです！
English: You have to be sure to examine evidence carefully on all sides!

Japanese: さ。さ。調べてみましょうよ！ いろんな角度から！
English: Now. Let's start examining! From every angle!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。そこ！ 何か手がかりがありますよ！
English: Oh look! I think there might be a clue there.

Japanese: さっそく、《調べる》ボタンに タッチしてみてください！
English: You should check it out! Press the Examine button.

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥ＩＤカード、かな。
English: This... This is an ID card.
(Detective Bruce Goodman,

Japanese: ほら、ほら。どうですか！ ベンリでしょ？　カガク捜査。
English: See? Well? Isn't scientific investigation useful?

Japanese: そうだね。‥‥カガクは あまりカンケイない気がするけど。
English: I guess... Though I don't see what "science" has to do with it.

Japanese: 《多田敷捜査官のＩＤ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Goodman's ID added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品を見つけたら いろいろ調べてみましょうね！
English: Let's be sure to examine every piece of evidence we find!

Japanese: （なんというか‥‥ 気が抜けなくなったな）
English: (I guess I've got to be on my toes from now on...)

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと。
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: よろしかったかしら。 そこの、おふたかた。
English: Were you two all set?

Japanese: あ。あたしたちですか？
English: Us?

Japanese: （なんだ？　まさか‥‥）
English: (What's this? She couldn't be...)

Japanese: お、お弁当屋さん、かな。 ‥‥殺人現場なのに。
English: Y-you're selling lunches? Here? This is a crime scene!

Japanese: はい。こちら、とりそぼろ弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: Hello! Half n' Half, was it?

Japanese: あ、ど。どうもスミマセン‥‥
English: Oh. Uh, thanks.

Japanese: そちらの殿方も。
English: And you, sir?

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: ノリ弁当でよろしかったかしら？
English: Some Crunchy Goodness coming at you!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥ （ぼくももらっちゃったよ‥‥）
English: Uh... thanks. (Interesting way of doing business.)

Japanese: こちら、関係者以外は 立入禁止ですのよ。
English: This area is off limits to anyone without clearance.

Japanese: ‥‥特に、一般のカタは。 おわかりだったかしら‥‥？
English: Especially passers-by. Or are you officers?

Japanese: あ。でも、あなたも、その。 カンケイ者には見えませんけど。
English: Uh... no, but you... You don't exactly look like the type to have clearance.

Japanese: ふう‥‥。これはまた、 ずいぶんなゴアイサツですわねえ。
English: Well, that's hardly a way to greet someone!

Japanese: この“ゲロまみれのおキョウ”も 今はムカシ。はかないユメ‥‥
English: Even if my days as the "Cough-up Queen" are over...

Japanese: げ、“げろまみれ”‥‥？ あ、あなたが？
English: C-cough-up? Huh?

Japanese: あ、あたし。おなかいっぱいだし、 やっぱり、このお弁当は‥‥
English: You know, I'm feeling kind of full. Maybe I'll pass on lunch...

Japanese: ワタクシ、この件には深い ゆかりがございますのよ。
English: I'm quite connected to this case, you see.

Japanese: 今も、目に焼きついていますの。 あのツヤっぽい、ヒメゴトが‥‥
English: The images are burned into my eyes, you might say. Yes, all the sordid secrets...

Japanese: ‥‥ひめごと‥‥？
English: Secrets...?

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 じれったいコムスメだねえ。
English: Dear me. You are a slow one, aren't you?

Japanese: 捜査官が刺された、 あの事件に決まってるだろうが。
English: I'm referring to the murder. The stabbing of that detective.

Japanese: え‥‥‥ええええッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥私、犯行の瞬間を ハッキリ見られましたので。
English: A witness clearly saw me committing the crime.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥お姉ちゃんが 言ってた“目撃者”って‥‥
English: You mean you're the witness my sister was talking about?

Japanese: あの！　詳しく聞かせてください、 ゲロまみれさん！
English: Please! Cough-up Queen! Tell us what happened!

Japanese: 市ノ谷　響華 （いちのたにきょうか）ですわ。
English: The name is Angel Starr. Don't you go forgetting it.

Japanese: ‥‥以後、お見知りおきを。
さもないと‥‥おキョウのカカトに むせび泣くコトになるだろうさ。
English: Or before you know it I'll have you whimpering at my heels.

Japanese: は、ははは、はいッ！ （なんだ、このムチャな迫力は！）
English: Y-y-yes, ma'am! (Yipes! She means it!)

Japanese: ここは‥‥クルマの出口ですね。
English: This is where the cars leave the lot.

Japanese: 床のヤジルシを見ると、 “入口”みたいだけどね。
English: The arrow on the ground makes it look more like an entrance.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ どう見ても出口じゃないですか！
English: What are you talking about? It's plainly an exit!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥出口であると同時に、 入口でもある、というコトかな。
English: Well, maybe it's both. Kind of a dual purpose?

Japanese: あ！　それ！　あの有名な “ソータイセー理論”ですね！
English: Ah hah! The theory of relativity!

Japanese: え！　は、はあ‥‥
English: What? Uh...

Japanese: メモしとかないとね‥‥ あ！　ね。ね。成歩堂さん。
English: I've got to write this down. Ah! Hey, hey, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: どこの国のヒトでしたっけ？ ソー・タイセーさんって。
English: Maybe you know... Was Mr. Relativity, German? Or was he British?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないよ。
English: Mr. Relativity? Are you sure that was his name?

Japanese: あ。ハシゴですよ。
English: Ah hah! A ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: Um... That's a stepladder.

Japanese: どこが違うんですか？ カガク的に証明してください。
English: What's the difference? In scientific terms, please.

Japanese: か、カガク的‥‥
English: S-scientific huh?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ましょうよ、 成歩堂さん。
English: Look at the basic nature of things, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なぜだろう‥‥あまずっぱい キモチになってきた）
English: (This all seems so horribly familiar somehow...)

Japanese: お。こんなところに イミありげなドアが！
English: Look! A door! This must mean something!

Japanese: ドアに“イミ”なんてないよ。
English: I'm not sure that doors "mean" anything.

Japanese: クッ！　開きません！ ナゾのカギがかかってます！
English: No! It won't open! A mysterious lock!

Japanese: カギに“ナゾ”なんてないよ。
English: I fail to see what's mysterious about it.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。 もっと人生を楽しみましょうよ。
English: Mr. Wright... You need to learn to enjoy life more.

Japanese: 先に調査をすませようよ。
English: Let's finish our investigation first, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥あんなところに、ガラス張りの オシャレな部屋があります！
English: Look! A stylish, glass-walled room! Very nice.

Japanese: あそこからだったら、駐車場を カンゼンに見わたせるね。
English: You could see the whole parking lot from in there.

Japanese: “ＳＥＣＵＲＩＴＹ”‥‥ね。 キッサ室でしょうか。
English: It says "SECURITY." Perhaps it's a cafe?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥キッサ・セキュリティ、と。 あとで、行ってみましょう！
English: "Cafe Security"... Yeah, that must be it. Let's check it out later!

Japanese: ザンネンだけど‥‥たぶん 警備室だぞ、あれ。
English: Um. I hate to break it to you, but I think that's probably just a security guard office.

Japanese: あ。あたし、こないだのテスト、 カガクは９７点でしたから！
English: You know, I scored a 97 on my science test the other day!

Japanese: （英語は何点だったんだよ‥‥）
English: (Too bad they don't have a test for common sense.)

Japanese: ドラム缶だ。 ‥‥水が入ってるみたいだ。
English: An oil drum. Looks like it's filled with water.

Japanese: お、重いです！ さすがにこれは。あたしでも。
English: I-it's heavy! I can't even budge it!

Japanese: ‥‥こっちのドラム缶は、 倒れてるね。
English: The drum over here is on its side.

Japanese: あ。あたし、この中に入って “はりこみ”しましょうか！
English: Wait! I know! I'll hide in here and do a stake-out!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなところに入ってたら タイホされちゃうよ。
English: I think you'll probably just get arrested.

Japanese: （ただでさえアヤシイのに）
English: (In fact, you may not even have to hide in the drum to get arrested.)

Japanese: アヤしくないっ！
English: What? I'm not suspicious!

Japanese: ちょっとジャマですね。 ‥‥このカベ。
English: This wall is in our way.

Japanese: 小さな蛇口がついてるね。水道の。
English: It's got a faucet for water.

Japanese: あ。もしかして、コレ！
English: Wait! I know!

Japanese: “カベ”は世をしのぶ 仮のスガタで‥‥その正体は！
English: This "wall" is merely a facade, hiding the truth...

Japanese: 非常用のタンクかもしれません！
English: This is no wall, but a water tank!!!

Japanese: （‥‥どっちでも いいんじゃないかな）
English: (I fail to see how it makes any difference either way...)

Japanese: お。電話がありますよ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Here, a phone. Let's see if it works...

Japanese: こらこら。 勝手にさわらないほうがいいよ。
English: Hey. Don't touch stuff we don't need to be touching.

Japanese: な、何も聞こえません！
English: I can't hear anything!

Japanese: あたしの耳、ヘンになったのカモ！ 気圧のせい、でしょうか‥‥
English: My ears! No, my ears! Maybe it's due to the barometric pressure...

Japanese: （ナニを、バカなコトを‥‥）
English: (What is she babbling about?)

Japanese: あ、そこ！　なんですか！
English: Hey!? What did you just say?

Japanese: 聞こえてるじゃないか！ 電話がコワれてるんだよ！
English: See? You can hear just fine. The phone's broken!

Japanese: ワタクシ‥‥知っていたような 気がしますの。
English: Somehow... I knew.

Japanese: 毎週、金曜日の日替わり弁当が シャケであるように‥‥
English: Yesterday was a day of destiny... I knew something was going to happen...

Japanese: きのうこそは、何が起こっても おかしくない‥‥ウンメイの日。
English: Just like I know that the Daily Special on Friday every week is salmon.

Japanese: “ウンメイ”‥‥あの。 何かトクベツな日だったんですか？
English: Destiny...? Was yesterday special for some reason?

Japanese: ‥‥そちら、弁護士でしたわねぇ。 ならば、ご存じなのでは？
English: You're a defense attorney, right? You should know then.

Japanese: ここに巣食うツミ深い殿方たちの、 非道のかぎりをつくした悪行。
English: You should know the foul misdeeds of the evil ones who haunt this den of inequity!

Japanese: あ、あくぎょう‥‥
English: E-evil ones?

Japanese: ヒトに罪の汚名を着せるため、 手段を選ばない‥‥それが、検事。
English: Prosecutors! They have no qualms at all about blackening the name of innocents!

Japanese: きのうは‥‥そう。最も邪悪なる 者をたたえる日でしたのよ。
English: And yesterday they paid homage to the most evil one of all!

Japanese: “優秀な検事”を、表彰する‥‥ まさに茶番、ですわね。
English: They gave an award for "King of Prosecutors"... What a farce!

Japanese: カンタンに言うと‥‥
English: So, she's saying...

Japanese: 検事さんたちの大会が あったみたいですね。きのう。
English: There was some sort of prosecutor's convention yesterday.

Japanese: お弁当に、カラダに悪いオカズを 入れようか、迷いあぐねましたわ。
English: I was almost compelled to lace their lunches with something foul...

Japanese: あの。どうして検事さんのコト、 そんなふうに言うんですか？
English: Do you have a personal grievance against prosecutors, or something?

Japanese: 検事さんがワルモノだっていう、 カガク的な証拠でも‥‥
English: Or is there some kind of scientific evidence of this, um, "evil"?

Japanese: ‥‥おじょうちゃん。
English: Young miss...

Japanese: このおキョウ、 ナメるとゲロの味がしますわよ。
English: Mock me at your own risk! You'll soon find out why they call me the "Cough-up Queen"!

Japanese: え！
English: Ew!

Japanese: きのう表彰された、 この辺で最もコッケイなオトコ‥‥
English: The most heinous of all the evil ones, the one they awarded yesterday...

Japanese: 被害者の遺体が発見されたのは、 その検事のクルマだったのさ。
English: It was in his car that they found the body!

Japanese: ヤツらが、極悪の弁当をむさぼり 食ってる、何よりの証拠さね！
English: Proof that he devours the evilest lunches of all!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなんですか！
English: R-really!?

Japanese: （なんなんだろう‥‥）
English: (Really what? I'm totally confused...)

Japanese: （このひとの、“検事”に対する 憎しみのようなものは‥‥）
English: (One thing's clear. This lunch lady has a thing against prosecutors.)

Japanese: それで、その。響華さんが 目撃した、というのは‥‥？
English: So, what exactly was it that you witnessed, Ms. Starr?

Japanese: それは‥‥ナマめかしいながめ、 でしたわねえ。
English: It was a fascinating spectacle, to be sure!

Japanese: オンナの情念‥‥そのようなものを 感じてしまいましたわ。
English: I now feel I know what they say when they talk about a "woman's wrath."

Japanese: あの、宝月　巴が ナイフを使った瞬間に。
English: To see Lana Skye wield that knife so...

Japanese: 怒りのタンザク切り、悲しみは イチョウ切りのナイフさばき‥‥
English: Her knife flashed in anger, bringing him to a sad end.

Japanese: それはそれはミゴトなモノ、 でしたわねぇ‥‥
English: It was truly a sight to see.

Japanese: ま、まさか！ おキョウさん、犯行の瞬間を‥‥？
English: Y-you mean you saw the very moment of the crime!?

Japanese: イノチの赤い糸が千切りにされる マナイタの音‥‥今も、この耳に。
English: The sound of his silvery ties to this world being cruelly cut still rings in my ears.

Japanese: 宝月　巴の赤い糸を刻む 包丁のリズムも、ここまでだねぇ。
English: And the rhythmic beat of Lana Skye's knife...

Japanese: あの‥‥あなたは、 宝月　巴さんを知ってるんですか？
English: Wait a second! You know Lana Skye?

Japanese: 主席検事サマ、かしら？
English: Hmph. Of course. It's quite a feat... becoming Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: どれだけの罪を重ねて、 その名をつかんだコトか‥‥
English: How many lunchboxes of sin did she pack to make that journey, I wonder!

Japanese: に‥‥２段がさね‥‥
English: She... always travels light.

Japanese: （キレイな弁当屋のお姉さんが、 なぜ主席検事の名前を‥‥？）
English: (Now why would this pretty lunch lady know the Chief Prosecutor's name?)

Japanese: あの。おキョウさんのコト、 聞かせていただけますか？
English: Um... Could we ask you a bit about yourself, Ms. Starr?
I come here every day to sell lunches.

Japanese: オンナもこのトシになると‥‥ ひとつやふたつ、昔話があるもの。
English: I import only the freshest and best from the Far East.

Japanese: ‥‥オコサマには 聞かせられないような、ねぇ。
English: For some reason, the box lunches are a hit here.
Why not make the lunches here rather than import them?
Did you say something?

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: N-no...
Only true conossieurs can understand...

Japanese: くさや弁当の味がわかるように なったら、またいらっしゃいな。
English: The kind you can only tell someone who has tried General Tso's Trilobite lunch set.

Japanese: ま、まいりましたっ！
English: Ah... Nevermind... You win.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥クサいな）
English: (I don't even want to appreciate part of a trilobite's flavor.)

Japanese: ワタクシ、毎日ここへ お弁当を売りにまいりますの。
English: Anyway, I come here every day to sell lunches.

Japanese: この検事局の守衛室に、 カレがいるんですのよ。
English: My boyfriend works in the security room here at the Prosecutor's Office.
Y-your boyfriend?

Japanese: ほら。あそこに、 守衛室が見えますでしょ？
English: See the security room over there?

Japanese: “ＳＥＣＵＲＩＴＹ”と書かれた、 あのガラス張りの部屋ですか。
English: The glass-walled booth?

Japanese: お弁当を売りに来るついでに、 カオを出してさしあげますの。
English: I sell my lunches and, since I'm here anyway, I drop in to see him.

Japanese: （カレのほうは“ついで”か）
English: (Since you're here anyway... I guess selling lunches is more important than romance.)

Japanese: とりあえず、これまでの情報を カガク的に整理すると‥‥
English: So, to scientifically analyze the data available so far...

Japanese: おキョウさんは、何かワケありの お弁当屋さん、というコトですね！
English: You, Ms. Starr, are a lunch vendor with an ulterior motive for coming here!

Japanese: （‥‥役に立たない情報だな）
English: (Useful analysis. Not.)

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥検事局で、 何かあったんですか？
English: Did you have a bad experience with a prosecutor, Ms. Starr?

Japanese: 強い“悪意”のようなものを 感じるんですけど‥‥
English: I sense some... hostility.

Japanese: 悪意‥‥ そうかもしれないわねぇ‥‥
English: Hostility? Hah! Perhaps.

Japanese: 検事なんて、みんな同じ。 エラいヤツほど、始末が悪い‥‥
English: Prosecutors are all alike. And the bigger they get, the worse they smell.

Japanese: シジミのおみおつけに、あえて アワビを入れるようなものかしら。
English: Kind of like 10-day old clams in the chowder.

Japanese: （おキョウさん‥‥ムカシ、 事件にまきこまれたのかな）
English: (I wonder if Ms. Starr was involved in some sort of legal trouble in the past?)

Japanese: あ。きっと、食中毒がらみですよ！ カガク的に考えて。
English: That'd be a sure cause of food poisoning! Scientifically speaking, of course.

Japanese: だって、なんと言っても 《ゲロまみれ》だし！
English: I mean, now you're talking "Cough-up Queen"!

Japanese: （おキョウさん‥‥いったい、 何をしていたヒトなんだ‥‥？）
English: (I thought she was just a lunch vendor, but now I'm not so sure...)

Japanese: 弁護士は、たたかうオトコ。 そちら‥‥勝てるのかしら？
English: A defense attorney must be able to fight...
How about you? Do you think you can win?

Japanese: ベントーランドがこだわりぬいた、 自家製タクアン弁当の歯ごたえに！
English: How about tackling Lunchland's Pickle Supreme lunchbox!?

Japanese: うん！　カリカリしてて、 とってもオイシイですっ！
English: Wow! It's really crunchy!

Japanese: （‥‥こだわりぬくのはいいけど、 さみしいオカズだな）
English: (A box of pickles...? Kind of a sad lunch if you ask me.)

Japanese: あの。このカードなんですけど。
English: About this card...

Japanese: ベントーランドは、現金のみ。 カードは扱っておりませんの。
English: Lunchland vendors only accept cash. No cards.

Japanese: しかも、他人サマのカードは。
English: Especially not a card belonging to someone else!

Japanese: い、いやいや。これ、捜査官の ＩＤカードみたいなんですけど‥‥
English: No, no, this isn't a credit card. It's an ID card. It belongs to a detective...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなものを弁当屋に 見せられてもねぇ。
English: And you're showing this to me, the lunch lady, why?

Japanese: 捜査官に、ハマチの切り身を 見せるようなものですわ。
English: That's like showing a fine honeyed ham to a detective!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、 バカにされているような‥‥）
English: (Why do I always feel like I'm being mocked?)

Japanese: これ、見ていただけますか？
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥そちら。
English: You!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!?

Japanese: アガリは、ほうじ茶で よろしかったかしら。
English: You said you wanted some hot tea, right?

Japanese: あ、どうも‥‥ （見る気はないみたいだな‥‥）
English: Uh, no, but thanks. (She didn't even look at me.)

Japanese: うん。とってもオイシイ！ 深炒りでしか出ない味ですよねー。
English: Mmm! You must have to brew the leaves a long time to get rich flavor like this!

Japanese: 炒ったときのケムリで、 茶葉をいぶしてますのよ。
English: We "pre-infuse" the leaves with steam before brewing.

Japanese: やっぱり！　香ばしさのヒミツは そこにあったんですねー！
English: I knew it! So that's the secret to their aroma! Exquisite!

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ、 この香ばしい空気は‥‥）
English: (The only thing I'm smelling here is wasted time...)

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 警察局・エントランス
English: February 22 Police Department Entrance

Japanese: うー‥‥ やっと着きましたね。
English: Whew... We're finally here.

Japanese: なんでこんなに検事局から離れた トコに建てるかなあ、警察局。
English: Why would they put the detectives so far away from the Prosecutor's Office?

Japanese: タクシーで３０分近くかかったね。
English: That took almost 30 minutes by taxi... and traffic wasn't even that bad.

Japanese: いつも刑事課に行くから、 警察局に来るのは初めて‥‥ん？
English: This is my first time to the Police Department, actually.

Japanese: なんだ？　アレ。
English: Hold on, what's that?

Japanese: ぶ‥‥ブキミですね、なんか。 ワキワキとうごめいてますよ。
English: Disturbing! Why does it undulate like that?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。コイツならたしか、 “タイホくん”とか言ったっけ。
English: Oh, wait I know. This is the "Blue Badger."

Japanese: 将来、警察のマスコットに なるかもしれないよ。
English: They're trying to make him the police mascot.

Japanese: うわ。成歩堂さん、 ケーサツマニアですねー！
English: Wow, Mr. Wright! You know a lot about the Police Department!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。なんか、どこかで 見たことがあるような‥‥
English: Still, he does seem familiar, somehow...

Japanese: それより‥‥ タイホくんの、となりだけど。
English: Forget the Blue Badger! Who's that next to him!?

Japanese: タイホくんと、いっしょに おどってるヒトがいますね‥‥
English: Someone appears to be... dancing with the Blue Badger...

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 あたしたちに気がついたみたい。
English: Uh oh. He noticed me.

Japanese: スゴいイキオイで こっちに走ってくるな‥‥
English: He sure is running over here fast...

Japanese: あ。あ。あ。あ。アンタ！ こ。こ。こ。こ。ここで何を！
English: H-h-h-hey, pal! W-w-w-what're you doin' here!?

Japanese: それは、こっちのセリフですよ イトノコ刑事。
English: That's my line, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: どうしてこんなトコロで おどってるんですか？
English: Specifically, why were you dancing over there?

Japanese: えッ！　それは‥‥その。
English: What!? Um, well...

Japanese: （せっかくだ。ヒマみたいだし、 いろいろ聞かせてもらおう）
English: (Well, at least he doesn't seem to be busy. This is our chance to get information!)

Japanese: ヒマじゃないッス！
English: Hey! I'll have you know I'm a very busy man, pal.

Japanese: まあ。自分が話せるのは、 こんなところッスかね。
English: And that's all I know about that.

Japanese: なにぶん、 事件を担当してないッスから。
English: I'm not officially on the case, you know.

Japanese: ありがとうございました！
English: Thank you!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、イトノコ刑事が担当して いないなんて、めずらしいですね。
English: Why aren't you handling the case, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: 地下駐車場の担当のヒト‥‥ なんて言いましたっけ？
English: We met the guy who is... what was his name? The guy in the parking lot...

Japanese: ザイモン巡査ッスか。
English: That'd be Officer Marshall.

Japanese: 警察局長、じきじきの 任命ッスから‥‥勝てないッス。
English: He was appointed directly by the Chief of Police...

Japanese: ミスター・サイモン‥‥ やっぱり、アメリカから？
English: Officer Marshall... Is he some kind of Wild West sheriff or something?

Japanese: いやいや、ザイモンッス。 罪門　恭介（キョウスケ）。
English: No, Jake Marshall's just a regular officer...

Japanese: リッパなニッポン人ッス。
English: From West LA.

Japanese: え！　そ、そうなんですか！
English: For a moment there, I wasn't sure.

Japanese: まま。ここはヒトツ、自分が ベンギをはかってあげるッス。
English: Look, pal, let me try to make things a little easier for you.

Japanese: これを見せれば、現場を調べさせて もらえるッスよ。‥‥たぶん。
English: Show them this and they'll let you examine the crime scene... maybe.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の《紹介状》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Letter of Introduction from Detective Gumshoe added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （この刑事さんに、そんな チカラがあるのか‥‥？）
English: (I'll be surprised if this gets us anywhere...)

Japanese: でっかいドロ舟に乗った気分で ひとっぱしり、行ってくるッス！
English: Just act like you're supposed to be there, and nobody will look at you twice, pal!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 アレのコトなんですけど。
English: Uh, I was wondering about that...

Japanese: 《おどるタイホくん》ッスか。 ‥‥ズバリ、自信作ッス。
English: What? The Dancing Blue Badger? It's my masterpiece!

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事が 作ったんですか、コレ。
English: You made this, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: カチョー殿の設計図をもとに、 自分がウデをふるってみたッス。
English: The chief threw together some designs and I just did my thing, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥へえ！
English: N-nice work...

Japanese: どこでも置けるように、 デンチで動くようにしてあるッス。
English: It's battery-powered, so it can go anywhere!

Japanese: スイッチがないから、デンチが 切れるまで、おどり狂うッス。
English: There's no switch, so it just dance dance dances until the batteries die!

Japanese: カナしい運命なんですね、 タイホくん‥‥
English: Poor Blue Badger... fated to dance until he drops.

Japanese: 《タイホくん》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Blue Badger added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 刑事さんたちが見える。 みんな、忙しそうだ。
English: The detectives in there look pretty busy.

Japanese: あのドアの向こうで、 今、まさに！
English: Just imagine! Right now...

Japanese: くり広げられているんですね。 ‥‥“刑事ドラマ”がッ！
English: Behind those doors...! A police drama in action!

Japanese: （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
English: (Somehow the thought fails to excite me...)

Japanese: 掲示板には、おなじみの ポスターが張ってある。
English: The usual wanted posters are hanging up on the bulletin board here.

Japanese: 《このカオに、ピン！　と来たら １１０番》‥‥か。
English: «Do you know this face!? If you do, dial 911!»

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 こういうポスターのカオって‥‥
English: You know, Mr. Wright, I've always thought it was kind of funny...

Japanese: なーんか、 ピン！　と来ないんですよねー。
English: I've never seen anyone who looked like the people in these posters.
They hardly even look human!

Japanese: （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
English: (She has a point...)

Japanese: 《真冬の防犯フェア・開催中！》 ‥‥って、書いてあります。
English: The banner here is announcing the "Crime Fighting Campaign."

Japanese: 大ざっぱなスローガンだな。
English: Nice slogan...

Japanese: ルミノール試薬とか、 安く売ってないかなあ。
English: I wonder if they'll be selling fingerprinting sets.

Japanese: ‥‥バーゲンセールじゃ ないからね。
English: I don't think it's that kind of campaign.

Japanese: 家庭に１本あると、 安心だと思うけどなあ。
English: What family wouldn't want a set at home?

Japanese: ‥‥サツジン事件が 起きたとき、とか。
English: It's good for finding out who snuck into the cookie jar.

Japanese: 安心のためなら、他のものを 買ったほうがいいと思うよ。
English: I think most families can figure that out without the extra help.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　パトカーの色、 なんでシロクロか知ってますか？
English: Mr. Wright! Do you know why patrol cars are painted black and white?

Japanese: 知らないよ。どうして？
English: No idea. Why?

Japanese: あたしね。アレ、おむすびを デザインしたんだと思うんですよ。
English: Well, I think they're designed after a panda!

Japanese: お、おむすび‥‥？
English: A panda...?

Japanese: まあ。カガク的なコンキョは ありませんけどね。
English: Not that I have scientific proof. It's just a theory.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、 どうしてそう思うの？
English: Um... do you mind me asking how you came up with that theory?

Japanese: 小学生のころ、遠足で見学に来た とき、パッとヒラめいたんです！
English: It was when I was on a school trip! I saw a patrol car and it came to me!

Japanese: ‥‥お弁当の時間に。
English: We had just been at the zoo, see...

Japanese: 今度、パンダを見に行くときは、 サンドイッチにしたほうがいいよ。
English: What about zebras? Or did they not have those at your zoo?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　警察のマーク、 どういうイミか、知ってますか？
English: I always get excited when I come to the police station.

Japanese: 知らないよ。なんなの？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: あたしね。コレ、宇宙ステーション だと思ってたんですよ。ムカシ。
English: It just feels like I've jumped into a movie.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 小さいコロの話ですけど。 ホラ、カタチが似てるでしょ？
English: You know, with all the police and criminals.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。見たことないから。 ‥‥宇宙ステーションは。
English: W-well, I don't know if this is all that exciting.

Japanese: でも、“それはあり得ない”って 気づいたんです。去年。
English: Sure it is! Look at those two officers over there.

Japanese: だって、“ポリスステーション” ですからね。“警察署”は。
English: They're probably talking about the latest bust!

Japanese: もっと早く気づきたかったね。 それも、別のカタチで。
English: Funny, I thought they were talking about the weather.

Japanese: おまわりさんがケーレイしている。 相手は刑事さんだろうか。
English: Look, that patrolman is saluting the other guy. He must be a detective!

Japanese: そこで、オレは言ってやったのさ。 《カツ丼‥‥さめるぜ》ってな。
English: And then I said "hey, you do that, your soup will get cold, buddy."

Japanese: い‥‥いいハナシでありますッ！ 本官、泣けてきましたッ！
English: Th-That's hilarious, sir! I laughed so hard I cried!

Japanese: ケーレイじゃなくて、 ナミダをふいてるみたいですね。
English: I guess he wasn't saluting, he was wiping tears from his eyes.

Japanese: ‥‥いいコンビになりそうだ。
English: They make a good pair.

Japanese: とにかく、アンタに１つだけ 忠告しておくッス。
English: I'll give you one word of advice, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女のベンゴだけは、 引き受けちゃダメッス。
English: You'd better not agree to defend the suspect in this case.

Japanese: ど。どうしてそんなコト 言うんですかッ！
English: Wh... Why not?

Japanese: え！　えーと、その。主席検事殿は ハッキリ犯行を認めているッス。
English: Huh?
Well... It's just that the Chief Prosecutor has confessed to the crime.

Japanese: 被害者の捜査官を検事局に 呼び出して‥‥殺害したと。
English: She says she summoned the detective to the Prosecutor's Office and... she killed him.

Japanese: でも！　それがホンネかどうか、 わからないじゃないですかっ！
English: But, what if she's not telling the truth!?

Japanese: い、いやその。決定的な証拠も たくさんあるッスし。
English: Yes, well... no! C'mon, pal! There's plenty of evidence against her!

Japanese: でも！　そんなの、デッチ上げ かもしれないじゃないですかっ！
English: B-but what if the evidence was faked?

Japanese: あの‥‥アンタ。 ちょっといいッスか？
English: Hey, pal. Can I speak to you for a second?

Japanese: はい？　ぼくですか。
English: Huh? Me?

Japanese: なんで自分、こんなちっちゃい子に 怒られなきゃならないッス？
English: Why is this little girl so peeved at me?

Japanese: その子、妹さんなんですよ。 ‥‥宝月　巴さんの。
English: She's a relative of the suspect. Lana Skye's sister.

Japanese: うげへェェッ！ 主席検事殿のォォッ！
English: Whoa!!! The Chief Prosecutor's little sister!?

Japanese: ちゃんと捜査してくださいね！ いわば、その。カガク的にっ！
English: Just, please investigate this case carefully, okay? Scientifically!

Japanese: はッ！　‥‥あ、そッス。
English: Yessir! Oh, by the way.

Japanese: ここであんまり大きな声で 言わないほうがいいッスよ。
English: You might want to keep your voices down.

Japanese: ‥‥“デッチ上げ”なんて。
English: You don't want to be overheard using words like "faked"...
Huh?

Japanese: ワレワレは今、そのコトバに ビンカンになってるッスから‥‥
English: It's just... it's a sensitive issue with us these days.

Japanese: それで‥‥何してるんですか？ こんなトコロで。
English: So... what are you doing here, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: いや、その。なんというかあの。
English: Me? Oh, well... nothing, really.

Japanese: 追い出されちゃったッス。 ‥‥刑事課を。
English: They kicked me out of Criminal Affairs...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥また、 何かやっちゃったんですか？
English: Detective Gumshoe! What did you do this time?

Japanese: なな、なんスか、“また”とは！
English: Whaddya mean, "this time"!?

Japanese: じゃあ、どうしたんですか？
English: Then, what happened?

Japanese: 今、タイヘンなんですよね？ その‥‥お姉ちゃんのコトで。
English: I know things are busy right now...
I mean... with my sister's case and all...

Japanese: そッスね。主席検事の殺人なんて 前代未聞ッスから。
English: It's true. We've never had a Chief Prosecutor murder anyone before!

Japanese: 今、刑事課に入れるのは、刑事部長 よりエラいヒトたちだけッス。
English: Only the highest-ranked people are being let into Criminal Affairs now...
The lowest ranking guy in there is our chief of detectives.

Japanese: ワレワレ、ヒラの刑事は‥‥その。 まぜてもらえないッス。
English: They're not letting any of us rank-and-file detectives in at all.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: None of you?

Japanese: （いくら主席検事の裁判でも‥‥ さすがに不自然じゃないか？）
English: (I know this is an important trial, but isn't that a little odd?)

Japanese: とりあえず、自分は今日中にこの おどりをマスターしようかな、と。
English: So, anyway, I thought I'd spend the day getting the badger dance down pat.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。他にするコトは ないんですか？
English: Um... Isn't there anything else you could be doing?

Japanese: 事件の捜査は、警察局長殿が ご自身で指揮をとってるッスし‥‥
English: The Chief of Police himself is directing this investigation, pal.

Japanese: 検事局の地下駐車場の捜査には、 ザイモン巡査が任命されたッス。
English: And Officer Marshall was assigned to the underground parking lot.

Japanese: ‥‥ザイモンさん‥‥
English: Officer Marshall...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥茜ちゃん、 知り合いみたいだったな‥‥）
English: (Now that I think about it, Ema did seem to know that Marshall guy.)

Japanese: 巡査が現場の指揮を任される‥‥ これまた、前代未聞ッス！
English: A patrolman in charge of a crime scene... It's unheard of, pal!

Japanese: このＩＤカード‥‥ 被害者のものだったんですね。
English: So... this ID card belonged to the victim?

Japanese: 彼は、自分と同じく捜査官‥‥ いわゆる、刑事だったッス。
English: He was a detective, like myself.

Japanese: 多田敷　道夫 （タダシキミチオ）巡査部長。
English: Detective Bruce Goodman.

Japanese: うーん‥‥ なんか、ちょっとフシギですね。
English: Hmm... Don't you think it's strange?

Japanese: 被害者のＩＤカードが、どうして あんなところに落ちてたのか‥‥
English: I mean, why would the victim's ID card be lying on the ground where we found it?

Japanese: きのうは、多田敷刑事は 警察局にいたはずッスよ。
English: Well, Detective Goodman should have been at the Police Department yesterday.

Japanese: ２年前に担当した事件の証拠品の 《申し送り》があったッスから。
English: There was an evidence transferal for a case he handled two years ago.

Japanese: 《申し送り》‥‥御剣検事さんも そんなコトを言ってましたね。
English: Evidence transferal... Mr. Edgeworth mentioned that too.

Japanese: でも‥‥多田敷さんが 殺害されたの、検事局でしたよね。
English: But... Detective Goodman was killed at the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: だから‥‥その。 言いにくいッスけど‥‥
English: Well, that's the thing... It's hard to say this, but...

Japanese: 宝月主席検事殿が呼び出したッス。 ‥‥地下駐車場に。
English: Word is that Chief Prosecutor Skye called him out there, to the parking lot.

Japanese: （ともえさんも、それを認めてる ってワケか‥‥）
English: (And Lana's confessing as much...)

Japanese: ツラい立場に立たされているッス。 ‥‥今も、なお。
English: He's in a tough spot, again...
"Again"...?

Japanese: キッカケは、あの事件ッス。 生倉弁護士の殺害事件‥‥
English: Well, it all started with the murder of that defense attorney, Hammond.

Japanese: でも！　御剣検事さんの無実は 立証されたじゃないですか！
English: But Edgeworth was found innocent!

Japanese: 検事には、ムカシから イロイロなウワサがあったッス。
English: Listen, pal, there have always been rumors about Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥証拠のねつ造や、 証人との取引とか‥‥
English: Forging evidence, making deals with witnesses...

Japanese: 今までは、カゲでひそひそ ささやかれていたッスが‥‥
English: Nothing outright, but there were always whispered rumors.

Japanese: 殺人事件の容疑者になってから、 みんな、もうエンリョなしッス。
English: Ever since he was accused of murder, no one's whispering. They're practically shouting!

Japanese: そんな！　証拠もないのに！
English: But... but there's no evidence against him!

Japanese: ジッサイ御剣検事は、上層部との つながりが、不自然に強くて‥‥
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth has always had unusually strong ties to the department higher-ups.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、怪しまれても しかたないというか‥‥その。
English: It's only natural that people would be suspicious.

Japanese: （‥‥そこまで苦しい立場に いたのか、アイツ‥‥）
English: (I had no idea he was under the gun...)

Japanese: 今回の件も、そうッス。
English: Anyway, this latest case has started a new rumor.

Japanese: 『この法廷を引き受けたのは、 主席検事の座を狙っているから』
English: People say the only reason he took this case...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなウワサも あるぐらいッス。
English: is because he's aiming for the Chief Prosecutor position himself!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: ‥‥でも！ 自分にはわかっているッス！
English: But I know the truth, pal!

Japanese: 宝月主席検事の法廷なんて、 ダレもやりたくないッス！
English: Nobody wants to be the one who has to prosecute the chief prosecutor!

Japanese: 御剣検事は、あえてビンボークジを 引いたッス。みんなのために‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth is biting the bullet on this one! He's doing this for all of us!

Japanese: 刑事さん。ほら、これ。 弁護士がつけるバッジですよ。
English: Detective... here's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、会うたびに 見せるッスね、それ。
English: You show this to me every time we meet, pal.

Japanese: 男はだまって警察手帳ッス！
English: Real men show their police badge. 'Nuff said!

Japanese: いいなあ。あたしも 早くほしいな、身分証明‥‥
English: I wish had a badge... Even an ID card would be nice...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Wait...)

Japanese: （ぼくも持っていたっけ。 刑事さんのＩＤカード‥‥） I...?)
English: (Speaking of ID cards, I found that detective's card, didn't

Japanese: あの‥‥イトノコ刑事。 これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Um, Detective Gumshoe? What can you tell me about this?

Japanese: お！　アンタ、コレ‥‥ 捜査官のＩＤじゃないッスか！
English: Huh? Hey, pal! This is a detective's ID card!

Japanese: ダメッスよ！　拾ったら、 ちゃんと交番に届けないと。
English: You can't just keep that! You have to turn it in to the police!

Japanese: アンタみたいなのがいるから、 いつも自分、怒られちゃうッス。
English: It's people like you that get me into so much trouble all the time!

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事‥‥いつも 落とすみたいだな、ＩＤカード）
English: (Meaning Detective Gumshoe must drop his card a lot.)

Japanese: えー、なになに。 “タダシキ　ミチオ”‥‥
English: Hmm... let's see... "Bruce Goodman"...

Japanese: タダシキ‥‥？ 最近、どこかで聞いたような。
English: Goodman... Sounds familiar...

Japanese: 気のせいッスね、きっと。
English: Nah, my mistake.

Japanese: でも、刑事課でいっしょに シゴトをしてい
English: But, don't you work together with him in Criminal Affairs?

Japanese: うほオオオオオオオオオオオッ！ 気のせいじゃなかったッス！
English: Whoa!!! Now I remember!

Japanese: 多田敷　道夫といえば‥‥ 今回の事件の被害者ッスゥ！
English: Bruce Goodman! He's the victim!

Japanese: （‥‥なんとなく、 そんな気はしてたんだよな）
English: (That's what I thought...)

Japanese: け、刑事さん！ お話、聞かせてくださいっ！
English: Can you tell us more, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: 事件の被害者・多田敷刑事の ＩＤカードッス。
English: This ID card belongs to the victim, Detective Bruce Goodman.

Japanese: 今日び、なんでもカードに暗証番号 ‥‥気が休まるヒマがないッス。
English: You can do just about anything these days with a card and the right secret number... scary!

Japanese: それは、イトノコ刑事が ぽろぽろ落とすからですよ。
English: It's only scary because you keep dropping your card, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 暗証番号もぽろぽろ忘れるッス。 ‥‥自分がコワくなるッス。
English: I forget my secret number a lot, too. It's scary!

Japanese: ダレが見たって、自分は自分！ それでいいじゃないッスかッ！
English: But... but I'm me, after all! And what could be wrong with that!?

Japanese: （開きなおった‥‥）
English: (I think I'll stay out of this conversation.)

Japanese: お、それ！
English: Hey, that's it!

Japanese: きのう御剣検事がもらった 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》ッス！
English: That's the "King of Prosecutors" award that Mr. Edgeworth got yesterday!

Japanese: 授賞式、イトノコ刑事も 出席していたんですか？
English: Were you at the awards ceremony, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: そりゃそッス！ 自分ももらったッスよ。努力賞。
English: Of course, pal! I got an award for diligence, myself.

Japanese: はあ‥‥おめでとうございます。
English: Ah... congratulations.

Japanese: それにしても、 なんで《盾》なんですか？
English: I was wondering, why is the award a shield?

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、割れてるし。
English: And... why is it broken?

Japanese: それはモチロン、 ちゃーんと理由があるッス。
English: Oh, there's a reason.

Japanese: ええと‥‥‥‥‥‥ また、今度教えてあげるッス。
English: Um... I'll tell you what it is later.

Japanese: （忘れたらしいな、どうやら）
English: (Apparently, he's forgotten.)

Japanese: ‥‥でも。御剣検事が 受賞して、自分もうれしいッス。
English: But, I was proud of Mr. Edgeworth for winning that award.

Japanese: 検事局では、彼を認める声 ばかりじゃないッスからねえ。
English: He's even got naysayers in the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （“黒いウワサ”‥‥か）
English: (Yeah, we've heard about the rumors...)

Japanese: 御剣検事のクルマに、 御剣検事のナイフ‥‥
English: Found in Mr. Edgeworth's car, stabbed with Mr. Edgeworth's knife, huh...

Japanese: どうして宝月主席検事殿は、 そんなコトをしたッスかねえ‥‥
English: What would drive Chief Prosecutor Skye to do such a thing?

Japanese: あ。あやや。も、モチロン！ ハンニンは、ベツのニンゲンッス！
English: W-wait, I didn't mean... I mean, sure, of course someone else really did it!

Japanese: そのハンニンは、まるで‥‥
English: Someone who must have, um...

Japanese: 御剣検事に、ウラミでも あるみたいッスねえ‥‥
English: Someone who must have a grudge against Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ グーゼンとは思えないよな）
English: (The car and the knife do seem a little too well-organized to be a coincidence.)

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥ いったい、何があったんだろ‥‥
English: Poor Mr. Edgeworth... What could have happened?

Japanese: （詳しく聞いてみたいな。 アイツのこと‥‥）
English: (We have to find out a little more about what's going on with Edgeworth...)

Japanese: イッピキの刑事として、 これだけは断言しておくッス！
English: As a detective, I have to keep my mouth shut on that one.

Japanese: ココロ当たりのないモノには、 断固！　チンモクを守るッス！
English: I know better than to go blabbing on about things I don't know about...

Japanese: か、かまいませんよ、それで。
English: No, I wouldn't want you to do that either...

Japanese: ‥‥ホッ。
English: Good.

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 22 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: （‥‥捜査はまだ つづいてるみたいだな）
English: (Looks like the investigation is still going...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥お店に戻るわね。
English: I have to be getting back to the shop...

Japanese: 今日は、この荒野にホネを埋める コトになりそうさ。
English: Sorry... Looks like I'll be stuck in this pit 'til the sun sleeps.

Japanese: 今夜はユメで会うとしようか、 ベイビイ。
English: I'll see you in my dreams tonight, then, baby.

Japanese: ‥‥あら。 またいらしたの。
English: Oh! Still here?

Japanese: あ、ど。どうも‥‥
English: Ah, h-hello.

Japanese: どうしたのかしら、その目。 ‥‥言いませんでしたかしら。
English: Why the surprised looks? Didn't I mention...?

Japanese: ワタクシ、捜査官にもカレが いるんですのよ。
English: I've got a boyfriend in Criminal Affairs, too.

Japanese: （“守衛室のカレ”は なんだったんだよ！）
English: (What happened to the security guard!?)

Japanese: ヘイ、どうしたバンビーナ。
English: Hey! What's wrong, bambina?

Japanese: カギをなくしたカギっ子‥‥ そんなセツない目、してるぜ。
English: You're lookin' like a dogie that's lost its herd!

Japanese: （罪門　恭介‥‥）
English: (Jake Marshall...)

Japanese: （たしか、この現場の セキニンシャ、だったよな）
English: (Strange guy to put in charge of a crime scene.)

Japanese: あの‥‥ どうですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: So, well, how are we doing, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 手がかりは、そろってきたかな。
English: I guess we've got some clues...

Japanese: 解剖記録に、被害者のメモに‥‥ 携帯電話、か。
English: We have an autopsy report, a note from the victim, and a cell phone...

Japanese: よかった‥‥
English: So... you think we'll be okay?

Japanese: 気になるのは‥‥ともえさん自身が 犯行を認めてるコトだね。
English: Well, the only thing still bothering me is that Lana is confessing to the crime.

Japanese: 『私がやった』って。
English: She says she did it!

Japanese: 大丈夫ですよ！
English: No problem!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんがハンニンじゃないのは あたしが保証しますから！
English: I can guarantee that she's not the criminal.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、茜ちゃん。
English: Oh by the way, Ema?

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: きみの持っている携帯電話の 着信メロディ‥‥知ってるよ。
English: I know that song your phone plays when it rings...
What...?

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《大江戸戦士トノサマン》か。
English: It's the Steel Samurai theme song, isn't it?

Japanese: 今、ヤングに大人気のヒーロー‥‥ なんだってね。
English: That popular TV show... for kids?

Japanese: さっき鳴ったのは、ぼくのデンワ じゃなかった。
English: The phone that just rang wasn't mine...

Japanese: そう。 ‥‥きみのだよ。あかねちゃん。
English: it was yours.

Japanese: 宝月　巴さんは、 事件が発生した直後‥‥
English: At 5:18, just after the murder took place...

Japanese: きみにデンワをかけた。 ‥‥そうだね？
English: Your sister called you, didn't she, Ema?

Japanese: ご‥‥ごめんなさい‥‥あたし。
English: I...I'm sorry!

Japanese: 聞かせてくれないの？ お姉さんと、何を話したか‥‥
English: Can you tell me what you talked about?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 すぐ、切れちゃったから。 I...
English: She hung up right away.

Japanese: そう、か‥‥わかった。
English: I see...

Japanese: 証拠品《携帯電話》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Cell Phone added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （殺害されたのは、現職の捜査官。 被告人は、検事局のナンバー１）
English: (A detective is murdered, and the suspect is the top prosecutor in the district.)

Japanese: （もしかしたら、ぼくは‥‥）
English: (I've got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: （まだ、この事件のすべてを 知らないのかもしれない‥‥！）
English: (Like... maybe I still don't know everything that went on here...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: おっと。そいつを調べるのなら、 オレを倒してからにしなよ。
English: If you want to take a closer look, you'll have to deal with me first, pardner.
Huh...

Japanese: 夕陽の決闘‥‥シカバネを越えた オトコだけが、真実をつかむ。
English: A duel at sunset... The winner leaves the loser in the dust and takes all.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、ガンマンたちの 血のオキテ、なのさ。
English: The blood law of the gunman!

Japanese: ‥‥捜査官のナワバリ争いは 撃ち合いで決着をつける、と。
English: Note to self: jurisdiction squabbles are settled by dueling.

Japanese: （殺人事件の調査なのに、死体を 増やしてどうするんだよ‥‥）
English: (I think one body per murder case is enough, thanks.)

Japanese: これは‥‥メモ、かな。
English: What's this? Looks like a note of some sort.

Japanese: あ！　何か書いてありますよ！
English: Look! Something's written on it!
You're right. Let's see...

Japanese: 上に、名前が印刷されてますね ‥‥タダシキ、って。
English: There's a name printed on the paper above that... "Goodman."

Japanese: （殺害されたときに、 ポケットから落ちたのか‥‥）
English: (Maybe it fell out of his pocket when he was killed.)

Japanese: で？　で？　どういう イミなんですか？　成歩堂さん！
English: Well? So? What does it mean, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: わかるワケないだろ。
English: How am I supposed to know?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士と御剣検事さんの “差”は、このへんにあり、と。
English: Note to self: for deductive reasoning, go to Edgeworth, not Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥御剣でも わからないと思うけど）
English: (I'm sure Edgeworth wouldn't know what this means either.)

Japanese: 《多田敷のメモ》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Goodman's Note added to the Court Record.

Japanese: これは‥‥携帯電話だね。
English: This looks like a cell phone.

Japanese: カガク的に考えて‥‥被害者のもの じゃないでしょうか！
English: Scientific analysis would suggest this belonged to the victim!

Japanese: ‥‥他に、思いつかないし。
English: I can't think of anyone else it could belong to...

Japanese: （どこがカガク的だよ！） とにかく、調べてみようか。
English: (What's so scientific about that!?) Should we check it out?

Japanese: やっぱり、やめておこう。
English: On second thought, let's not.

Japanese: えー！　どうしてですか？ 気になるじゃないですか！
English: What!? Why not? I mean, don't you want to know whose it is?

Japanese: でも、たまたま刑事さんが 落としたものかもしれないよ。
English: Probably one of the Detectives dropped it.

Japanese: 警察には、そんなマヌケな 刑事さんはいませんっ！
English: Come on! No detective would be that dumb!

Japanese: （今、一瞬ダレかのカオが アタマをよぎったような‥‥）
English: (I don't know. One detective in particular comes to mind.)

Japanese: （よし‥‥ 詳しく調べてみよう！）
English: (Right! Let's check it out.)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥これ以上の 手がかりは見つからないな）
English: (I think that about does it for clues here.)

Japanese: 電話のスクリーンを見ると‥‥ リダイヤル表示になってますね。
English: Hmm. The display is still on the redial button.

Japanese: リダイヤル‥‥？
English: Redial...?

Japanese: はああ‥‥あのね、成歩堂さん。
English: Um, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 最近の携帯電話には、 発信と着信の記録が残るんです。
English: Most phones keep a record of all the calls you've made and received.

Japanese: 青いボタンを押せば、最後にかけた 番号を呼び出してくれるんですよ。
English: You just press the blue button to dial the last number you called.

Japanese: ベンリになったモンでしょ？ 成歩堂さんが知らないうちに。
English: Convenient, isn't it? I'm surprised you didn't know about it.

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけど、リダイヤルぐらい 知ってるよ、ぼくも。
English: Sorry to disappoint you, but even I know about things like "redial."

Japanese: え！　ご、ごめんなさい！
English: Huh? Oh, I'm sorry!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、ひと時代前のヒトに 見えるから！
English: It's just, you never know with people from your generation.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 もう少し、調べてみるか）
English: (Whatever... Let's check this phone out.)

Japanese: （さてさて。 ダレにかかったものか‥‥）
English: (Now, I wonder who the owner of this phone called last...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士は、あとさき考えず とりあえずボタンを押す、と。
English: Note to self: a defense attorney doesn't think first, he just pushes the button.

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: うわ！　こ、このメロディは‥‥
English: Hey! That song! I know that!

Japanese: ヘイ、そこ！　どうしたッ！
English: Hey! What's going on over there?

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: あ！　いや。すみません。
English: Ah! Oh, s-sorry.

Japanese: 見てたぜ、カウボーイ！
English: I see you, pardner!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、今、そのデンワで リダイヤルをしたな？
English: You pressed redial on that there phone, didn't you?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥まあ。
English: Uh, well, yeah...

Japanese: こ。このデンワ、何なんですか？ そこに落ちてたんですけど。
English: Whose phone is this, anyway? It was on the ground over there...

Japanese: ‥‥そいつはな。 宝月　巴主席検事のモノさ。
English: Whose is it? That belongs to Chief Prospector Skye.

Japanese: えッ！　お、お姉ちゃんの‥‥？
English: What? It's my sister's!?

Japanese: 犯行直後、身柄をカクホされたとき 落としたらしい。
English: She apparently dropped it when she was taken into custody, right after the crime.

Japanese: 発信記録によると‥‥どうやら、 事件が起こった、まさにその時間。
English: Look... the last call was made right when the murder occurred!

Japanese: だれかにデンワをかけたようだよ。 ‥‥通話時間は数分だがね。
English: Looks like she was fixing to call someone.
Except she only spoke for a few seconds, according to this.

Japanese: ‥‥！　いったい、ダレに！
English: Who did she call!?

Japanese: それが‥‥わからなかったのさ。
English: No idea. Sorry, pardner.

Japanese: だから、カウボーイ。 質問に答えてもらえるかい。
English: Now, I got a question for you, pardner.

Japanese: たった今、鳴った電話は、 いったい‥‥
English: I heard a phone ring just now... one of those new- fangled ring-tunes.

Japanese: いや‥‥‥その。すみません。 ぼくのデンワだったんですけど。
English: Oh? That? Oh... I'm sorry, that was my phone.

Japanese: な、な、な‥‥なんだと‥‥？ アンタの‥‥デンワ‥‥！
English: W-w-what!? Your phone!?

Japanese: なんというか、 言いにくいんですけど‥‥その。
English: Yeah, uh, it's kind of strange, but...

Japanese: いいタイミングで、かかってきた みたいです。‥‥まちがいデンワ。
English: Someone called me right as we picked up the other phone, a wrong number...

Japanese: ここがテキサスなら、アンタ‥‥ 弾丸を噛んでるところだぜ！
English: I hope you're not lying... They shoot you for that in Texas, pardner!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ 怒って行っちまったよ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, I've incited the wrath of the Lone Star patrolman...)

Japanese: 証拠品《携帯電話》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Cell Phone added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥これが、死体が 発見されたクルマ、みたいだね。
English: This appears to be the car where the body was found.

Japanese: （トランクのカギが コワされているみたいだな）
English: (It looks like the lock on the trunk is busted.)

Japanese: 現場は、地方検事局の地下駐車場。
English: The crime took place in the underground parking lot at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 遺体は、私の部下のクルマの 後部トランクから発見されました。
English: The body was found in the trunk of my subordinate's car.

Japanese: いかにも“カネ持ち”的な ニオイがしますねー。
English: Quite a luxury car. It just screams "I have money to burn."

Japanese: ううん‥‥ もうかるんだなあ、検事って。
English: Yeah. Prosecutors get the big bucks.

Japanese: 《Ａブロック》‥‥ 検察関係者のエリアですね。
English: "A Block"... This area is reserved for prosecutors.

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士だから、 《Ｂブロック》になるのかな。
English: Defense attorneys are relegated to "B Block."

Japanese: いつかあたし、ここに クルマをとめるのがユメなんです！
English: I dream of the day when I will be able to park my car here!

Japanese: それで、Ｂブロックの成歩堂さん から、ハンバーグ弁当を買うの。
English: I'll go over to B Block to buy my hamburgers from you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥とうぶん、弁護士を やめるつもりはないよ。
English: I'm not planning on giving up my job that soon...

Japanese: この金網の向こうが《Ｂブロック》 ‥‥外来者用のエリアだね。
English: "B Block" is through there. That's where visitors park.

Japanese: あ。遠くにちっちゃく見えます！ ベントーランドのクルマ。
English: I can see the Lunchland car over there...far in the distance.

Japanese: ホントだ。ドアのワキに かわいい絵が書いてあるね。
English: Hey, you're right. I like the cute design on the door.

Japanese: （ウシのキャラクターが、ジツに ウマそうにステーキを食べてる）
English: (I can see... a cartoon cow munching down on a juicy looking steak.)

Japanese: そういうのって、なんか やるせない感じがしませんか‥‥？
English: Doesn't that strike you as a little... creepy?

Japanese: か、考えすぎだよ。
English: Just don't think too deeply about it and you'll be fine.

Japanese: このロープ‥‥やっぱり？
English: This rope... is it...?

Japanese: うん。被害者の死体のカタチを ロープでなぞってあるんだよ。
English: Yep. They laid it in the outline of the victim's body.

Japanese: 被害者‥‥トランクを閉めるとき、 挟まって死んだのでしょうか！
English: So wait...The victim must have died when the killer closed the trunk on him!

Japanese: （こんな発想をする子、 他にはゼッタイいないだろうな）
English: (You have got to be the only person I know that would come to that conclusion.)

Japanese: ここにあったんですよね。 ‥‥このメモ。
English: So the note was in here...

Japanese: 被害者・多田敷捜査官の ポケットから落ちたんだろうね。
English: It must have fallen out of Detective Goodman's pocket.

Japanese: で？　で？　どういう イミなんですか？　成歩堂さん！
English: And? And? what does it mean? Mr. Wright!

Japanese: わかるワケないだろ。
English: I have no idea.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士と御剣検事さんの “差”は、このへんにあり、と。
English: Note to self: for deductive reasoning, go to Edgeworth, not Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥御剣でも わからないと思うけど）
English: (I'm sure Edgeworth would be just as confused...)

Japanese: あの。聞かせていただきたい コトがあるんですけど！
English: There's something I wanted to ask you!

Japanese: ‥‥事件の現場は、そう。 ユメ破れたオトコたちの墓場さ。
English: The scene of the crime... a cold grave for men who've lost their dreams...

Japanese: オレはそれを見守る、ユメの番人。 砂漠の裁きをユメ見て眠るのさ。
English: And me? I watch over them as they sleep... dreaming of the desert's harsh judgment.

Japanese: 寝ちゃった。
English: He's asleep.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥このならず者の 注意をひくモノを見せてみようか。
English: Well... should we show this hopeless case something to catch his interest?

Japanese: あの、罪門さん。被害者のこと、 聞かせてもらえますか。
English: Officer Marshall? Could you tell us more about the victim?

Japanese: いいヤツは、みんな死んでいく。 これでいいかい、カウボーイ。
English: Good men always die young. Remember that, pardner.

Japanese: いやいや！ もう少し詳しくおねがいします。
English: Um... could you be a little more specific?

Japanese: 多田敷　道夫さん‥‥ 刑事さん、だったんですよね？
English: Bruce Goodman... He was a detective, right?

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥世話の焼ける 子馬ちゃんたちだね。
English: Well, well, aren't you a feisty dogie there now.

Japanese: 多田敷が刺されたのは、ここさ。 ‥‥５時１５分ごろのコトだよ。
English: Detective Goodman was stabbed here at 5:15...

Japanese: さすらうマドンナのほほえみが、 オレにそう物語っていたさ。
English: The smiling Madonna told me the tale...

Japanese: （おキョウさんの証言、か‥‥）
English: (I think he means the witness, Ms. Angel Starr.)

Japanese: 胸をひと突き。やっつけシゴトに しちゃあ、上デキの部類だろうね。
English: One stab to the chest. A fine piece of work.

Japanese: ‥‥コイツが解剖記録だよ。
English: This here's the autopsy report.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。お姉ちゃんと被害者は どういう関係だったんですか？
English: Was my sister involved with the victim in any way?

Japanese: それが‥‥妙といえば 妙なのさ、バンビーナ。
English: Funny you should mention that, bambina.

Japanese: 宝月主席検事と多田敷刑事には、 ほとんど接点がないんだよ。
English: Chief Prospector Skye and Detective Goodman... had nothing in common at all.

Japanese: 接点が‥‥ない‥‥
English: Nothing in common...?

Japanese: 何年か前に１度、いっしょに シゴトをしたことがあるが‥‥
English: They apparently worked together on a case a few years back.

Japanese: （つまり‥‥動機がない。 そういうことか‥‥！）
English: (So... there's no motive!)

Japanese: 多田敷は、とりたてて優秀な 捜査官でもなかったんだ。
English: Goodman wasn't a particularly gifted detective.

Japanese: 主席検事とシゴトできるチャンスが なかった‥‥そう言えるようだね。
English: That's one reason why he didn't do much work with the Chief Prospector...

Japanese: でも‥‥お姉ちゃんは事件当日、 被害者を呼びだしたんですよね？
English: But, my sister called the victim here on the day of the murder, right?

Japanese: ‥‥この駐車場に。
English: Here... to this parking lot?

Japanese: ああ‥‥そのようだ。 人間相手の闘牛をするために、ね。
English: So it seems. Like calling an unarmed man to a shootout at high noon.

Japanese: あの‥‥ こんなコト聞いてアレですけど。
English: Um, I don't mean any offense, but...

Japanese: 罪門さん、“巡査”なんですよね？ ‥‥“刑事”ではなくて。
English: Officer Marshall, you're a patrolman, right? Not a detective.

Japanese: おやおや‥‥ここがテキサスなら、 アンタ。銃殺されているところさ。
English: You callin' me out? They shoot you for that in Texas.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: これでも、２年前までは捜査官 ‥‥りっぱな“刑事”だったさ。
English: I was one of them fancy-shoed "Detectives" till two years ago, to tell ya the truth.

Japanese: あ！　そうなんですか。 （‥‥先に言えよ！）
English: Oh, really? (Now he tells me!)

Japanese: でも。“巡査”は捜査の指揮を とることはできないんじゃあ‥‥？
English: But, you're a patrolman now. So how can you be in charge of a crime scene?

Japanese: そのとおりだよ、バンビーナ。
English: Nothing gets by you, does it, bambina?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥なぜ。 巡査のあなたが指揮を？
English: So, why are you in charge?

Japanese: なあに。人手が足りてないだけさ。 オレは、ただの見張り番だよ。
English: No reason. We're just short on hands right now. I'm keeping an eye out in the meantime.

Japanese: あれ、オカシイなあ。
English: That's odd, though.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事さんは、ヒマ‥‥ 手があいてるようでしたけど。
English: Detective Gumshoe was saying he had nothing to do... nothing important, at least.

Japanese: かわいそうなブルドッグだよ。 ふるべきシッポが見つからない。
English: He's nothing but a sad ol' cowdog, that can't find his tail.

Japanese: あの、ミツルギといっしょに いるせいかもしれないね。
English: Maybe it's because he runs with that Edgeworth, eh?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣‥‥ですか？
English: Edgeworth...?

Japanese: あのブルドッグは、ハズされた。 ‥‥警察局長の決定さ。
English: That cowdog's been kicked out of this cattle run... by order of the Chief of Police.

Japanese: ちなみに本人は、ハズされたコトに 気づいてないみたいだけどね‥‥
English: Just, he don't realize it yet.

Japanese: （捜査からハズされている‥‥ イトノコ刑事が！）
English: (Detective Gumshoe, kicked out of the investigation!?)

Japanese: とにかく、接点はないんですよね！ お姉ちゃんと、多田敷捜査官‥‥
English: So, there's no connection between Detective Goodman and my sister!

Japanese: そのとおりさ。 しかし、まァ‥‥
English: That's correct, but...

Japanese: そいつをおぎなってあまりある、 証拠品のゴールドラッシュだがね。
English: There's a goldmine of evidence against her...

Japanese: それに‥‥明日の法廷に立つ検事が あのミツルギとくれば‥‥
English: And the prospector tomorrow is none other than Edgeworth himself...

Japanese: もう、運命は決まったよ。 バンビーナにはキノドクだけど。
English: I'm afraid your sister's fate is decided, bambina. Many condolences.

Japanese: ‥‥ザイモンさん！
English: Officer Marshall!

Japanese: なんだい、バンビーナ。
English: Yeah, bambina?

Japanese: どうして‥‥ そんな言い方をするんですか？
English: H-how can you say that!

Japanese: ザイモンさん‥‥ムカシは、 お姉ちゃんと‥‥
English: You and my sister, you were...

Japanese: （“ムカシ”‥‥ 何か、つながりがあったのか？）
English: (Is there something between this cop and her sister that I don't know about?)

Japanese: ‥‥悪かったね。たしかに‥‥ こんなコト、言うべきじゃないよ。
English: I apologize, bambina. Something must have gotten to me.

Japanese: 検事局に吹きおろす、このイヤな カラっ風のせいかもしれないね。
English: Maybe it's that dry wind that's a-blowin' through the Prospector's Office.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ここには、だれかの “悪意”がウズまいている‥‥）
English: (Dry wind or ill will, someone's up to something here... but who?)

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍の“疑惑”‥‥ 早いもんさ。もう、２年はたつよ。
English: Suspicions about Mr. Edgeworth have been flying around for nearly two years now.

Japanese: “証拠のデッチ上げ”や “証人との取引”ですか‥‥？
English: Forged evidence... arranging testimonies, you name it.

Japanese: ああ。勝つためには手段は選ばない ‥‥だからこそ、無敗だったのさ。
English: He was unbeatable because he did whatever it took to win.

Japanese: アンタに会うまでは、な。
English: Unbeatable that is, until he met you.

Japanese: でも‥‥ウワサはあくまでも ウワサじゃないんですか？
English: But rumors are just... rumors, aren't they?

Japanese: 検事さんの世界では、 証拠がすべてじゃないんですか！
English: These are prosecutors we're talking about! Evidence is everything to them!

Japanese: 御剣の疑惑。そのモトをたどって いくと‥‥ある人物にたどりつく。
English: If you follow the rumors about Edgeworth to their source, you find one person...

Japanese: ダレも、口に出すことはできない ‥‥アンタッチャブルの世界さ。
English: But... they're off limits. Untouchable, you might say.

Japanese: “ある人物”‥‥？
English: One person? Who?

Japanese: バンビーナ‥‥きみのお姉さんだ。 主席検事・宝月　巴。
English: Bambina...It's your sister. Chief Prospector Lana Skye.

Japanese: え！ ‥‥お、お姉ちゃんが‥‥
English: What!? My sister...?

Japanese: 御剣ヒトリでヤンチャな遊びが できるワケじゃない。
English: Edgeworth couldn't rustle all those cattle by himself.

Japanese: “取引”という名の弾丸を シリンダーにこめる人間がいる。
English: Some people load their guns with bullets, some people load them with "deals."

Japanese: バカな！　御剣が‥‥ そんなことをするハズがない！
English: What, you're saying Edgeworth was making deals to win trials?

Japanese: “弾丸のないところに銃声はない” ‥‥荒野の老人たちのコトバさ。
English: "Where there's gunshots, there's bound to be bullets."
That's what the old-timers say.

Japanese: どこかに、デカいヒミツがある。 みんな、気づいているのさ。
English: There's a big ol' secret hidden around here somewhere. Everyone knows it.

Japanese: （だから‥‥イトノコ刑事は 捜査からハズされたのか‥‥）
English: (Is that why Detective Gumshoe was taken off the case...?)

Japanese: （御剣に、イチバン近いところに いるから‥‥！）
English: (Did they target him because he was closest to Edgeworth?)

Japanese: えーと、ザイモンさん。 これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Officer Marshall? Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: おやおや‥‥カウボーイ。
English: Whoa, pardner!

Japanese: 武器をつきつける‥‥ここじゃあ そいつは決闘の合図だ。
English: If you're fixing to draw on me, you best be ready for a shootout!

Japanese: ハゲタカのエサになるカクゴが ある、ってワケかい？
English: You ready to become food for the vultures, compadre?

Japanese: そんなカクゴしてるんですか？ 成歩堂さん！
English: Are you ready, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥協力してくれる つもりはないみたいだな）
English: (Somehow I don't think he's going to help us.)

Japanese: あの‥‥これ、 読んでもらえますか？
English: Would you mind reading this for me?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだい、コイツは。 ファンレターはタンツボにたのむ。
English: What's this? I warn you, fan letters to me go right in the spittoon!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の《紹介状》です。 捜査、させてもらえませんか？
English: It's a letter of introduction from Detective Gumshoe. May we investigate?

Japanese: イトノコ‥‥ああ。 あのユカイなブルドッグか。
English: Gumshoe...? Ah, that old cowdog?

Japanese: ふうん‥‥バースデイ・パーティー でも、おっ始めるつもりかい？
English: Hmm... He holding a birthday party or something?
Huh?

Japanese: 見ろよ。《紹介状》のトコロが‥‥ 《招待状》になってるぜ。
English: Look, where it should say letter of "introduction"... It says "invitation."

Japanese: ‥‥‥か。 書きまちがいだと思います。
English: Ah... I think he just miswrote it.

Japanese: （なんでイトノコ刑事のぶんの ハジを、ぼくがかくんだよ！）
English: (Great, Detective Gumshoe. I owe you one...)

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろうさ。 他ならぬ刑事殿の血判状だ。
English: No worries. This proves it's from Detective Gumshoe, better than a blood test.

Japanese: 巡査ふぜいのオレとしちゃア、 聞かなくちゃならないだろうね。
English: Guess I'd better let you in, then.

Japanese: あ。ありがとう、ザイモンさん！
English: Th-thank you, Officer Marshall!

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Officer Marshall isn't a "detective"... he's a "patrolman"...)

Japanese: （罪門さんは“巡査”だ。 “刑事”じゃない‥‥）
English: (That reminds me of something...)

Japanese: あれ。ヘンだなあ。
English: That is odd!

Japanese: ‥‥事件現場のセキニンシャは、 刑事以上のハズなのに‥‥
English: Isn't a crime scene supposed to be handled by a detective or higher?

Japanese: ‥‥さて。 それでは、みなさま。
English: Well, folks.

Japanese: 手がかりがうなっている、 我らが金鉱へようこそ。
English: The clues are calling! Welcome to our gold strike.

Japanese: オレたちの合い言葉は、開拓精神。 せいぜい、楽しむとしようか！
English: Be like the settler! Strike out for lands unknown!
Manifest Destiny! Let's have a hootenanny!

Japanese: ‥‥かいたくせいしん、と。
English: Note to self: police investigations are like settling land.

Japanese: さ。成歩堂さん！ いっちょ、やりますよ！
English: Well, Mr. Wright, what do you say!?

Japanese: （コイツはもう、いらないな‥‥）
English: (I say I won't be needing this anymore...)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の《紹介状》を 丸めて捨てた。
English: Detective Gumshoe's Letter of Introduction crumpled and discarded.

Japanese: ほお‥‥アンタのバッジは まんまる、ってワケか。
English: I see your badge. Looks pretty... round.

Japanese: オレたちのバッジは、コイツさ。 夜空を照らす、スター‥‥星。
English: Our badge is a star, a lone star, shining in the nighttime sky.

Japanese: 闇にまぎれた、おたずね者‥‥ そいつを照らす、一条の光だよ。
English: A beam of light, illuminating evildoers who come in the dark of night!

Japanese: ‥‥おたずね者は お星さまの光に弱い、と。
English: Note to self: evildoers are weak against starlight.

Japanese: （‥‥それ、シェリフの バッジじゃないか！）
English: (Hey, that's a sheriff's badge!)

Japanese: そいつは‥‥ 警察官たちのパスポート、かい。
English: What's that? Some sort of police passport?

Japanese: 多田敷刑事のＩＤカードです。 ちょっとフシギなことに‥‥
English: This is Detective Goodman's ID card. Strangely enough...

Japanese: ずいぶん現場から離れたところに 落ちていたんですよ。
English: We found it a good distance away from the crime scene.

Japanese: ‥‥この国は、ジツにちっぽけだ。 テキサスの砂漠にくらべたらね。
English: Good distance, in this rat hole? If you want distance, get yourself to Texas!

Japanese: てきさす‥‥
English: Texas...

Japanese: そのちっぽけな国の、ちっぽけな 殺人現場の、ちっぽけな証拠品。
English: This is a tiny little crime scene, in a tiny little town, with tiny little evidence.

Japanese: たかだか数メートル離れていたから なんだって言うんだい？
English: What difference does a few yards make, compadre?

Japanese: ‥‥不自然な証拠に出会ったら テキサスを思い浮かべろ、と。
English: Note to self: if you encounter suspicious evidence, think of Texas.

Japanese: ベンキョウになりますねー。 プロのコトバは、やっぱり。
English: There's no better way to study than to hang out with the pros!

Japanese: 検事殿のまっ赤なじゃじゃ馬が、 トロットで入場してきたワケさ。
English: The prospector's bright red steed came in at a trot, real slow like.

Japanese: とろっと‥‥？
English: A... trot?

Japanese: オレのマドンナの証言によると、 犯行は、その３分後だった‥‥
English: My Madonna tells me the crime occurred three minutes later...

Japanese: どうやら、主席検事殿は 待っていたようだな。
English: So it seems the Chief Prospector was lying in wait!

Japanese: 赤い馬に乗った王子サマが お帰りになるのを‥‥な。
English: Maybe... waiting for her prince to ride in on his bright red horse...

Japanese: （つまり、ハンニンは‥‥）
English: (So what you mean is...)

Japanese: （最初から、御剣のクルマを 犯行に使うつもりだった‥‥？）
English: (The killer intended to use Edgeworth's car all along!)

Japanese: ‥‥ブリキのオモチャか。 お似合いだな、おぼっちゃまには。
English: Ah, a toy shield! Suits the boy well.

Japanese: そんなモノで、何が守れる？ オモチャの騎兵隊のタマぐらいさ。
English: What exactly could you shield with that? A toy knight, maybe?

Japanese: ‥‥罪門さん、さっきから 御剣検事のワルグチばっかり。
English: Officer Marshall, don't you have anything good to say about Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: まあ‥‥御剣のことがキライ なのは、よくわかったよ。
English: You don't like him, right? We get the point.

Japanese: たしか‥‥オレが刑事だったころ、 コイツをもらったことがあったな。
English: You know, when I was a detective, I got one of these...

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ “けいじ”の《Ｋ》ですか？
English: Hmm... Let me guess... Did it have a "K" for "King of Detectives" on it?

Japanese: 使い回してるぞ。
English: Hey, they could use the same shield over and over.

Japanese: ‥‥検事局と警察局は 意外にケチ、と。
English: Note to self: the Prosecutor's Office and Criminal Affairs are surprisingly cheap.

Japanese: まったくね！　ムカシよりも さらにケチになってるようだよ‥‥
English: You know it! They've gotten cheaper with every passing year, I tell you.

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃんの 携帯電話、ですね。
English: My sister's cell phone.

Japanese: 発信時刻は、５時１８分‥‥ 多田敷が殺害された直後だ。
English: The last time it was used was 5:18... Right after Goodman was killed.

Japanese: 夜のデートをキャンセル したのかもしれないぜ。
English: Maybe she was canceling her date for the night?

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥いったい、 なんのために電話なんか‥‥？）
English: (Why did Lana make that call...?)

Japanese: オーライ！ ‥‥３つ数えろ、カウボーイ！
English: Alright, compadre! Count to three!

Japanese: え！
English: Huh?

Japanese: テキサスで、ヒトに 武器をつきつけるってコトは‥‥
English: You gotta do that if you're going to draw evidence on someone.

Japanese: それは、決闘の合図なのさ。
English: That's what we do in Texas.

Japanese: その、テキサスのルールを 持ち出すの、やめましょうよ‥‥
English: Remind me never to visit Texas.
